item.title: 🚨 TWO HPD OFFICERS UNDER TEXAS RANGERS INVESTIGATION 🚨 According to newly released court records, the Texas Rangers ar...
item.description: 🚨 TWO HPD OFFICERS UNDER TEXAS RANGERS INVESTIGATION 🚨 According to newly released court records, the Texas Rangers are investigating two Houston police officers over allegations that a GPS tracking device was secretly attached to a vehicle without a warrant. Authorities are reportedly examining possible violations involving unlawful installation of a tracking device and official oppression. (ABC13 Houston) The investigation centers on a September 2025 surveillance operation in northwest Houston. Court records state that video footage allegedly captured HPD Officer Juan Gonzalez approaching a pickup truck and attaching a tracking device near the rear wheel well. The footage was later provided to investigators. (ABC13 Houston) Investigators say no warrant was obtained for the tracking device. The Harris County District Attorney’s Office reportedly confirmed that no tracking order was sought. (ABC13 Houston) Records show Sgt. Peter Vu received a 15-day suspension without pay, while Gonzalez resigned from HPD in April. Both officers initially denied involvement before later admitting they had not been truthful during an internal inquiry, according to court records. No criminal charges have been filed at this time. (ABC13 Houston) This case highlights a question that should concern everyone regardless of politics: If police believe they have enough evidence to track someone’s movements, why not obtain a warrant and let a judge review the facts? The investigation remains ongoing. #Houston #HPD #TexasRangers #Accountability #CorruptionWatchTexas

item.title: I don’t know any more about this situation than is presented here. However, it resonated with me because I have had SEVE...
item.description: I don’t know any more about this situation than is presented here. However, it resonated with me because I have had SEVERAL “good” (for lack of a better term) cops tell me their “associations” don’t have their back. I *do have a question. If you’re “protecting and serving” why do you need an “association” to attack your critics? 🤷After more than three decades as a member of TMPA(Texas Municipal Police Officers Association), I have decided to terminate my membership and requested removal from all associated databases. This decision stems from the organization’s social media representative’s handling of their platform. I have witnessed this individual engage in disparaging remarks towards citizens, exploit personal photos from their pages, and exhibit unprofessional behavior towards the community we serve. (Screen shots attached) Despite criticism, TMPA’s leadership has failed to address these issues, leading me to infer that they either condone or disregard such behavior. Consequently, this does not align with my values as a retired Texas Peace Officer. Kevin Lawrence Tyler Owen from TMPA called me. I suspect it was due to him getting multiple phone calls from all over the Houston area asking what the heck was going on. While I still don’t agree with Tyler about it being acceptable to go after citizens in a professional organizations feed, I give him credit for calling. He said he would take my concerns under advisement for future comments. I will say I think he is off the mark when he mistakenly equates personally attacking people who comment negatively with getting the facts around a members critical incident out. They are Very different things and you can do one without the other and when you do, it’s a professional look. Not something that looks like a teenager posts.

item.title: 🚨 ARRESTED‼️ ‼️Several arrest have been made!‼️ ⭐️ A sanitation worker... 🌳 A logger 🛻 A truck driver 🏠 A roofer 🐟...
item.description: 🚨 ARRESTED‼️ ‼️Several arrest have been made!‼️ ⭐️ A sanitation worker 🌳 A logger 🛻 A truck driver 🏠 A roofer 🐟 A commercial fisherman & 👮 A Police Officer Let’s say these mugshots were all posted by the local SO For 5 of these arrestees, MOST of the responses would be identical (“good jawb sheriffff! Thank ewe fer keeping us safe…”) with NO NEED FOR DETAILS. And let’s be honest, they’d attack anyone who asked for more details - ask me how I know. For ONE, they’d be “just one mistake… stressful job… another chance… blah blah blah” and of course “we need the rest of the story…” Can you guess which one? NOW here’s where it gets interesting. I made these mugshots of these very specific occupations. (We won’t mention how “occupation” only matters for one group) Why? Because EVERY ONE of the “non sacred” arrestee’s jobs have THREE things in common. ‼️#1 THEIR jobs are CONSIDERABLY more dangerous than the other one (and doesn’t “danger” = “stress”?) (also, if you remove self harm from the equation the “danger” for the one job goes WAY down - we’re talking like #25 “most dangerous” look it up) ‼️#2 YOU would suffer greatly if any of THOSE 5 professions just disappeared. They grow your food, deliver your food, harvest the material to build your house, cover that house you eat that food in, and haul away the trash you make eating that food! But no one acts like the World would end without them. No one worships them. They don’t get “discounts”, just for their job. They don’t get their asses kissed when they *do their actual job… etc. ‼️#3 THEY can’t destroy your life with the strike of a pen and they didn’t take an OATH!‼️ But, when *they get arrested, they are treated differently. In fact they are just plain treated differently. They don’t get lunch brought to them. Their crimes aren’t rationalized or justified by sheep. Does that make sense? This is “cop hatred” it’s the grown up view of a dire situation. We have to start having an honest, adult conversation about these things. Right? #corruption #watch #texas #accountability

item.title: ‼️Let me translate something for you‼️ 😇“If you aren’t breaking the law, you have nothing to worry about…” 🙄 Is the sa...
item.description: ‼️Let me translate something for you‼️ 😇“If you aren’t breaking the law, you have nothing to worry about…” 🙄 Is the same as saying: “Only criminals want privacy and to protect their rights…” It is the product of years of brainwashing, destruction of the spirit of self reliance and frankly, FEAR wrapped in fake virtue. It’s the weak cosplaying the strong 🤬PS - it’s also VERY insulting to those of us who have been maliciously targeted by the system. If you don’t know what that’s like, your self righteous pontifications are a LOT like a blind man describing his favorite nudie magazine. ‼️Resisting YOUR desire to be a sheep doesn’t make ME a criminal‼️ Tell me I’m wrong.

item.title: 🚨 FORMER MONTGOMERY COUNTY DEPUTY ARRESTED 👮 Former Montgomery County Pct. 3 Constable Deputy Louis Norman has been fi...
item.description: 🚨 FORMER MONTGOMERY COUNTY DEPUTY ARRESTED 👮 Former Montgomery County Pct. 3 Constable Deputy Louis Norman has been fired and arrested. ⛔️ Investigators say he misused law-enforcement databases for personal reasons. According to public reports, the complaint came from a former girlfriend. Investigators allege Norman accessed NCIC/TCIC information — police-only systems — for non-law-enforcement purposes. Police databases aren’t toys. They’re not for curiosity, personal disputes, or keeping tabs on people. If investigators are right and those systems were accessed because of a personal relationship, it’s just ANOTHER in a rapidly growing list of abuses of Rights by those who swore an Oath to protect them. I want to know: How many searches were run? Who was searched? Who audits those searches? How often are improper database hits caught? And what happens when they are? Credit where it’s due: Pct. 3 says they placed him on leave, referred it out, cooperated with MCSO and the DA’s Public Integrity Unit, and terminated him once criminal charges were moving. But a bigger issue remains: Government databases are not personal surveillance tools. They are not for exes. They are not for curiosity. They are not for jealousy. They are not for leverage. When the state gives someone access to private information, there’s trust. When that trust is broken for any reason, the relationship suffers. When it’s done for personal reasons, the relationship MUST END. Presumption of innocence applies. But public trust requires public accountability! #Corruption #watch #texas #abuse #accountability

item.title: ‼️ TAKE HIS F&8ING BADGE‼️ 🔥 It’s a lot to read but THIS is exactly (one of) the problem(s) I have with Flock. 👀I wat...
item.description: ‼️ TAKE HIS F&8ING BADGE‼️ 🔥 It’s a lot to read but THIS is exactly (one of) the problem(s) I have with Flock. 👀I watched the Ring doorbell footage of the PD’s VICTIM and the Officer says “you can’t get a breath of fresh air in this town without us knowing it” and then goes on to accuse the wrong person. What’s the point of that statement and then calling this woman a liar? If they had so much data, why didn’t they know her truck was somewhere else (meaning it couldn’t be where they said it was) during the crime? This isn’t a rookie either. You think he ever violated any he’s rights before with such reckless abandon? Nah… they always get caught the FIRST time, right? I’ll talk about this next week.

item.title: 🔥 HARRIS COUNTY FLOCK CAMERA CONTRACT RENEWED AFTER HEATED DEBATE 🔥 Political tensions boiled over in Harris County th...
item.description: 🔥 HARRIS COUNTY FLOCK CAMERA CONTRACT RENEWED AFTER HEATED DEBATE 🔥 Political tensions boiled over in Harris County this week as county leaders argued over renewing the Sheriff’s Office contract with Flock Safety — the company behind the growing network of automated license plate reader (ALPR) cameras used across the Houston area. 📌 What happened? • Harris County Commissioners approved renewal of the contract. • County Judge Lina Hidalgo abstained from the vote. • Dozens of residents spoke against the cameras, raising concerns about privacy, government surveillance, data security, and potential misuse of collected information. • Sheriff’s Office officials defended the program, stating it is used for specific criminal investigations and helped locate stolen vehicles, investigate fatal hit-and-run crashes, and assist in child abduction cases. (Houston Chronicle) 📌 The numbers: • The contract renewal is reported to be approximately $860,000 annually. (Houston Chronicle) • Harris County law enforcement oversees a network of nearly 500 cameras throughout the county, although many are owned by HOAs, MUDs, and other local entities rather than Harris County itself. (Houston Chronicle) 📌 The controversy: Supporters say the cameras are a valuable investigative tool that helps solve serious crimes. Critics argue that creating large-scale vehicle tracking networks raises legitimate privacy concerns and creates the potential for government overreach, regardless of who currently controls access to the data. (Houston Chronicle) ⚖️ The real question isn’t whether Flock cameras can help solve crimes. The real question is this: How much surveillance are citizens willing to accept in exchange for public safety? Reasonable people can disagree on that answer. What they shouldn’t disagree on is the need for transparency, accountability, auditing, and clear limits on how these systems are used. What do YOU think? 👇 Should Harris County continue expanding Flock camera use, or are we crossing a line? #CorruptionWatchTexas #FlockSafety #HarrisCounty #Privacy #Surveillance #PublicSafety #GovernmentAccountability

item.title: 🚨 I thought this was fake at first 🚨 ✅ FACT CHECK FRIDAY ✅ A Florida traffic stop is getting attention nationwide aft...
item.description: 🚨 I thought this was fake at first 🚨 ✅ FACT CHECK FRIDAY ✅ A Florida traffic stop is getting attention nationwide after a deputy cited a woman for allegedly holding a cellphone in her RIGHT HAND while driving. 😳 Thing is… She doesn’t have a right hand. According to court records and body camera footage released after the stop, the Palm Beach County woman repeatedly told the deputy she could not have been holding a phone in her right hand because she was born with a limb difference and does not have a right hand. Despite that, the citation was issued anyway. The citation carried a $116 fine and accused her of violating Florida’s wireless communications while driving law. She challenged the ticket. This week, the citation was dismissed at the request of the deputy who issued it, with court records indicating the case was dropped due to a lack of evidence. Here’s a question, when an Officer is OBVIOUSLY caught making up probable cause for a stop (he specifically said “right hand”), should that be treated as harshly as if a citizen had made a false report? For the victim here (and that’s how I see her) the situation is no different. We have a 4th amendment for a reason and when it is [mis]treated as we are seeing so often today, it’s a problem! No matter how much the “if you’re not doing anything wring” crowd would have you think otherwise. What do you think? Should officers face additional review when a citation is issued for something that appears to be physically impossible? #Accountability #BodyCam #TrafficStop #Florida #DueProcess #CorruptionWatchTexas

item.title: 🚨Update on the plight of Hays County Judge Becerra🚨 Judge Becerra lost his appeal to the State Commission On Judicial...
item.description: 🚨Update on the plight of Hays County Judge Becerra🚨 Judge Becerra lost his appeal to the State Commission On Judicial Conduct (SCJC) and his public admonishment has been upheld. This matters to us because Lavaca County Judge Keith Mudd has done something seemingly very similar by having his private company website address on his public Judge Facebook page. Mudd claims it’s a “campaign” page though the profile picture shows him in his robe, holding a gavel, sitting on the Bench, and his cover photo is the courthouse. I have filed a formal complaint with the SCJC. I’m very curious to see what they do. And of course ask “why”. Stay tuned #Corruption #watch #texas

item.title: Maybe the Weimar, TX City Council has competition… Some people seem to think the World is only as big as the space their...
item.description: Maybe the Weimar, TX City Council has competition… Some people seem to think the World is only as big as the space their ego occupies. Meanwhile their intellect is bringing up the rear. We really do have a problem with far too many of these little gangs of rogue servants.We made it home safely. Ladies and gentlemen, what i witnessed tonight in the City of Trinidad Tx City Hall was the most blatant and awe-inspiring (in a bad way) display of abuse of power, hubris, and government overreach I’ve ever even heard of outside of Athens, Tennessee, in 1945. What happened tonight should have shocked the conscience of everyone watching. What was said should have caused the ears of all listening to melt. I have never seen such willful acts of defiance to the rule of law and respect for constitutional principles of governance. We will be immediately filing for ex parte injunctive relief for our client, Judge Bivens, to protect her property interests and rights to employment. We will also be filing more criminal charges against certain government individuals responsible. Every lawyer in attendance and the mayor begged city council members to follow the law and they still ignored us. I’m beside myself wondering if it was a dream based on the sheer audacity of what the city council did. Meanwhile, the police chief begins his vacation tomorrow after a mere 6 months on the job after starting a shitshow.

item.title: This is analogous to how ONE TOXIC City employee can do so much damage. Trust is like a 10,000-gallon water tank. You c...
item.description:
This is analogous to how ONE TOXIC City employee can do so much damage. Trust is like a 10,000-gallon water tank. You can spend years filling it with clean water — honesty, good work, good intentions. But it only takes a single drop of poison to make everyone question the whole tank. That’s the cruel math of integrity: Purity is built slowly. Contamination happens instantly.

Right, Lavaca County Sheriff’s Office (whom I cannot tag for some reason)?🚨SAPD Officer Indefinitely Suspended After Multiple Women Report Inappropriate Conduct🚨 A San Antonio police officer has been indefinitely suspended after multiple women reported inappropriate conduct, including explicit text messages, unwanted physical contact, and intimidating behavior while he was assigned to SAPD’s mental health response team. The disciplinary order against Officer Christopher D. De Los Santos details allegations spanning from 2023 to 2025, including claims that he sent a sexually explicit message to a fellow officer, touched another officer without consent, and berated a Center for Health Care Services member after a crisis call. 📚Read more here: https://thehawkseyecn.net/…/sapd-officer-indefinitely-susp…/ #TheHawksEye #Texas #BexarCounty #SanAntonioTX #SAPD

item.title: 🔥 FOR SALE: 🔥 Since I bought A Mac Studio to edit and broadcast, I don’t need my MacBook Pro 16”, late ‘19 model with...
item.description: 🔥 FOR SALE: 🔥 Since I bought A Mac Studio to edit and broadcast, I don’t need my MacBook Pro 16”, late ‘19 model with 16 gigs RAM. MAKE ME AN OFFER (should be about $700 for the computer according to what I’ve seen.) Shipping should be about $30 I’ll include the Brydge and USB dock. Battery cycles = 173 (they are good for 1,000+ according to Apple) Specs are in the pictures. There is a small “dent” where my finger is. It’s hard to see but want to be up front about it. Ask me whatever questions you may have but all the info should be in pics.

item.title: 🔥 TOMORROW ON COFFEE WITH CORRUPTION WATCH 🔥 Before the headlines… Before the databases… Before Houston started offici...
item.description: 🔥 TOMORROW ON COFFEE WITH CORRUPTION WATCH 🔥 Before the headlines… Before the databases… Before Houston started officially tracking “Bodies on the Bayou”… There was Joe Campos Torres. In 1977, the body of a 23-year-old Vietnam veteran was recovered from Buffalo Bayou after a brutal encounter with Houston police — a case that helped ignite protests, riots, and decades of distrust. Tomorrow morning we begin tracing the timeline ALL the way back to the beginning: 📍 Buffalo Bayou 📍 “The Hole” 📍 The death that changed Houston history No conspiracies. This is historical investigation. ☕ Coffee With Corruption Watch 🕖 7:00 AM Central Thursday, May 28 #Houston #BodiesOnTheBayou #CoffeeWithCorruptionWatch #TexasHistory #BuffaloBayou #Accountability
